data.title: New Talisman Gold Mines To Issue 1.7 Million Shares in Partial Conversion of Convertible Note
data.description:
New Talisman Gold Mines said it will issue 1.7 million ordinary shares in a partial conversion of the convertible note debt security issued to Hamish Brown, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

The amount payable for the shares will be NZ$69,300 at NZ$0.042 per share.

A full conversion of the note to ordinary shares would contravene the threshold of 20% of its total issued share capital under the New Zealand Takeovers Code.

The balance of the note will continue on existing terms for the remainder of the term under the board's discretion.

Further conversion of the note to shares may occur during that period if possible within the current regulatory regime.

data.title: German Trade Surplus Below Estimates
data.description:
Germany's trade surplus fell to EUR 16 billion in January 2025 from EUR 20.7 billion in December 2024, below forecasts of EUR 21 billion, marking the smallest trade surplus since last October due to a fall in exports.

Exports dropped by 2.5% month-on-month (mom) to EUR 129.2 billion in January, marking the first contraction in exports in three months.

Meanwhile, imports grew by 1.2% to a 16-month high of EUR 113.1 billion.

data.title: Diversified Energy, FuelCell Energy, Tesiac Agree to Form an Acquisition and Development Company
Watches of Switzerland to Launch $32 Million Buyback
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

Watches of Switzerland said it is launching a share buyback of up to 25 million pounds ($32.3 million).

The U.K. watch retailer said Monday that it is looking at a number of investment opportunities, both organic and inorganic, that it will prioritize, but that it has financial resources to pursue those while returning surplus capital to shareholders.

The buyback will start immediately, Watches of Switzerland said.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: Norva24 Group Receives SEK6.6 Billion Bid from Apax Funds
data.description:
Norva24 Group agreed to a recommended cash offer from Apax Funds through Nordahl BidCo valuing the former at 6.63 billion Swedish kronor.

Nordahl BidCo offered 36.50 kronor per share in cash for each Norva24 share, reflecting a 58.7% premium to the Nordic underground infrastructure maintenance company's closing price on March 7, according to a Monday release.

Norva24 noted that 57% of its shareholders have agreed to the offer, with the acceptance period open from March 28 until May 6. Closing of the offer is subject to requirements and regulatory approval.

Apax Funds also said it will launch a compulsory purchase and a delisting process if it secures more than a 90% stake in Norva24.

data.title: U.K. Vinanz Mulls Dual Listing on Nasdaq
data.description:
By Michael Susin

Vinanz said it is in talks to explore a potential Nasdaq dual listing.

The London-listed bitcoin mining company, which holds U.S. and Canadian Bitcoin operations, said on Monday that it has engaged with Lucosky Brookman to explore the possibility.

The talks, however, are at an early stage, it said.

"The Nasdaq is the home of many of the world's leading Bitcoin companies and Spot Bitcoin ETFs, providing those entities with exposure to institutional investors and significant pools of growth capital," Vinanz chair David Lenigas said.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: Diversified Energy-FuelCell Energy-Tesiac Collaboration to Leverage Coal Mine Methane and Natural Gas for Off-Grid Data Center Power Projects >FCEL
Market Chatter: Sungrow Power Supply Chairman Calls for Electricity Futures Exchange
data.description:
Sungrow Power Supply Chairman Cao Renxian proposed the establishment of an electricity futures market to hedge from pricing volatility, Yicai Global reported Friday.

Cao, also a delegate to the National People's Congress, said the power futures market could be set up in China's energy-rich northwest or in the Yangtze or Pearl River Delta regions, the report said.

China could also add electricity futures products into existing exchanges, according to the report, citing Cao.

The integration of new energy sources into the grid also brings volatility to the market, the report said, citing Cao.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Monday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fall after a rough week on Wall Street, as Trump won't rule out recession

U.S. stock futures fell Sunday, as President Donald Trump offered little reassurance after the S&P 500 had its worst week since September.

Trump Declines to Rule Out Recession

In an interview that aired Sunday, the president demurred when asked if there were reassurances he could provide to businesses seeking more clarity on tariffs.

Tariff Wars Are Often Short. Their Legacies Aren't.

Economists fear that Trump's tariffs, once they take hold, could have unexpected effects long after he leaves office.

China Struggles to Shake Off Disinflationary Pressure as Trade Threats Loom

Consumer prices nosedived in February, ending a year of tepid price growth.

America's job market is eerily similar to the 1990s dot-com bubble - and, yes, it's a worry

March 20 marks the 25th anniversary of the dot-com bust. Could it happen again?

U.S. Bolsters Position as World's Top Arms Exporter

America has benefited from rising European spending, though President Trump's move to curb support for Ukraine is raising concerns about future purchases.

Stock Investors Go on Defense With Dividends

Some investors are sheltering from market swings in shares of companies with high payouts.

A Government Shutdown Could Create Short-Term Volatility. But It's Mostly Noise.

Tariffs aren't the only concern in Washington DC. A looming government shutdown could temporarily roil the markets too.

Tariffs Inflict Pain on Home Builders When They Can Least Afford It

Trade levies could add an extra $10,000 to the cost of building a home, likely coming out of builders' margins.

Trump's 'chaotic' policy approach is creating opportunity for investors

There could be "quite a shift in the world order," says Mike Cudzil, a portfolio manager at Pimco.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Monday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
Foxconn Builds FoxBrain, Its Own AI Model

The world's largest contract electronics maker said that aided by Nvidia it had built a large language model with reasoning capabilities, developed in-house and trained in four weeks.

7-Eleven Owner, Couche-Tard Financial Advisers Looking for Potential Buyers for Some Stores

Seven & i Holdings said in a letter to shareholders Monday that Couche-Tard recently agreed to jointly explore a potential sale of some stores prior to signing a takeover agreement.

Bally's Offers Financing Deal to Australia's Star

American betting and entertainment company Bally's has offered to provide embattled casino operator Star Entertainment with additional financing.

Is the chip sector having a meltdown? By this measure, it's mostly just Nvidia.

Broadcom and Marvell shares have been hit recently. But Nvidia alone is responsible for about three quarters of the chip sector's lost market cap in the weeks since the DeepSeek news.

AI Profits Are Still a Mystery. An IPO Filing Has Clues.

A new IPO filing from start-up CoreWeave suggests that every dollar put into the AI cloud is quickly paying off thanks to rapid growth rates in the business.

Tough to Digest? McDonald's and Yum! Brands Are at Record Highs Despite Consumer Concerns.

The two fast food stocks have surged this year. But can McDonald's and Yum keep satisfying investors' appetite for growth?

CVS's New Mini Stores Go All In on Medicine and Skip Everything Else

The national drugstore chain is preparing to open a dozen stores offering full-service pharmacies but very limited retail.

An Intel deal now seems less likely. These clues may hold the key to its future.

Investors appear to be now be discounting recent M&A speculation that Intel will split in two.

Robinhood to Pay $30 Million for Regulatory Violations, Finra Says

The regulator said Robinhood had violated "numerous" rules, including failing to respond to red flags for potential misconduct.

Activist Mantle Ridge Builds Over $1 Billion Stake in Tech-Services Firm Cognizant

The investor has been working behind the scenes at the tech-services firm and isn't currently planning to nominate directors.

data.title: Norse Atlantic Redelivers Three Boeing 787-8s
data.description:
Norse Atlantic Airways completed the redelivery of three Boeing 787-8 aircraft, according to a Monday release.

The aircraft were initially received by the Norwegian airline in 2022 and subsequently subleased on a dry-lease basis with four years remaining on their leases before redelivery.

The company expects a "significant" accounting gain from the transaction, which will be recognized in the first quarter of 2025.

data.title: Germany Industrial Production Beats Estimates
data.description:
Industrial production in Germany increased 2% month-over-month in January 2025, rebounding from a 1.5% decline in December 2024 and beating market expectations of a 1.5% increase.

The strongest boost came from the automotive sector (+6.4%), while the food industry (+7.5%) and machine maintenance and assembly (+15.6%) also contributed positively.

On the other hand, output in the manufacture of fabricated metal products contracted by 7.7%, and energy production fell by 0.5%.

The less volatile three-month-on-three-month comparison showed that industrial production stagnated in the period from November 2024 to January 2025.

On a yearly basis, industrial output declined by 1.6%.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: Uzbek State Miner Navoi Targets GBP4 Billion London IPO
data.description:
(Updates to add JPMorgan's statement)

Uzbek state miner Navoi Mining & Metallurgical Co. is targeting a London listing in 2025 with a potential valuation of 4 billion pounds sterling, Bloomberg News reported March 7, citing people familiar with the matter.

The gold miner is working with Rothschild & Co. as an adviser for the possible initial public offer, according to the people, who added that Citigroup Inc. and JPMorgan Chase could also be enlisted to work on the IPO.

The considerations are at an early stage and the plans may be delayed or changed, the people cautioned.

JPMorgan declined to comment when reached by MT Newswires. Navoi, Rothschild and Citigroup did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Huaiji Dengyun Auto-parts to Set Up Overseas Factories for 100 Million Yuan
data.description:
Huaiji Dengyun Auto-parts Holding plans to invest up to 100 million yuan to set up overseas factories, including a valve business in Thailand, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Saturday.

The factories will be set up through the Chinese automotive engine company's subsidiaries in Thailand, Singapore and other markets.

MAS Tightens Digital Payment Regulations
data.description:
The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has banned digital payment token services providers from offering credit or leverage to retail customers and restricted their ability to accept credit cards, according to a parliamentary speech last Wednesday.

MAS has issued a warning over the nature of cryptocurrencies and cautioned that regulatory measures are not enough to protect customers from losses.
